title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Language Clinic: Regular File Admission
summary: The command concatenates strings into paths, follows any symlink, accepts directories and devices, reads before checking size, and reports every failure as file not found. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the regular file admission increment in bounded file-admission policy. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-regular-file-admission — Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_paths_file_admission_3(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"

steps.why: Retrieving Regular File Admission through the concrete bounded file-admission policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Regular File Admission model · Theory Regular File Admission, keep Regular File Admission connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Regular File Admission model · Theory Regular File Admission, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Regular File Admission precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Regular File Admission model · Theory Regular File Admission, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 8935 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Regular File Admission model · Theory Regular File Admission, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Anything for which Path.exists returns true is a safe text file.
